changes.before: A cold cup sat within easy reach. Boards worked over for hours. Two sets of notation. Chalk on skin. Shoulders still square. No sign of struggle on robe, body, or room.
changes.after: A cold cup sat within reach beside boards worked over for hours, chalk on skin, shoulders still square, no sign of struggle anywhere in the room.

changes.before: Cyndia nodded. They pushed the smallest boat down together.
changes.after: Cyndia put both hands on the gunwale and shoved with her shoulder.

changes.before: The cold cup and worked pages showed how long the conversation had lasted.
changes.after: The cup had gone cold beside pages worked over long enough to grind chalk into the grain.

changes.before: An explanation here would only sound like pleading to be read differently. Jaina knew that too.
changes.after: Any explanation here would sound like a plea, and Jaina left it unsaid.

changes.before: They still knew how to move through danger together.
changes.after: They cleared the stair without speaking and without colliding once.

changes.before: Habit put them back into line, but the damage stayed in the spacing.
changes.after: They fell back into line by habit, but the spacing stayed wrong.

changes.before: Their new spacing stayed with them on the climb.
changes.after: On the climb back up Sylvanas kept a ranger's half-step too close to Jaina.

changes.before: There it was. The next fire was already ahead of them.
changes.after: Hearthglen was overwhelmed, and Stratholme was next.

changes.before: Then Sylvanas was through, sliding past the desk as Kel'Thuzad turned faster than his scholar's body promised.
changes.after: Then Sylvanas was through, sliding past the desk as Kel'Thuzad turned faster than his frame had suggested.

changes.before: Habit put them back into line, but the damage stayed in the spacing.
changes.after: They fell back into line by habit, but the spacing stayed wrong.

changes.before: Something colder than betrayal. A flaw.
changes.after: Colder than betrayal: a weakness she could not ignore.

changes.before: Sylvanas had her answer. Jaina remained Jaina—useful, dangerous, the partner who had kept pace with her from road to city to trap ground. But now there was one more fact attached to her: if the enemy spoke to the part of her that loved structure more than consequence, he could hold her in place longer than was safe.
changes.after: Sylvanas had her answer: Jaina was still useful and dangerous, but structure could hold her still longer than safety allowed.

changes.before: No panic. No surprise. The calm had held to the end.
changes.after: He showed neither panic nor surprise; the calm had held to the end.
Jaina wasted no motion and offered nothing she had not been asked for.

changes.before: Not a strike. A pressure at the back of the teeth, a searching drag along the skin.
changes.after: A pressure touched the back of her teeth and searched along her skin.

changes.before: There was no drama to it.
Not weakly. With the measured care of a body that had spent too long standing still in the wrong room.
changes.after: With the measured care of a body that had spent too long standing still in the wrong room.
